hero.title: FAQ
hero.desc: Can't find what you're looking for? Browse the questions below or reach out.

cta.title: Still have questions?
cta.desc: Send us your feedback via the form and we'll get back to you.

items.question: How do I create an account and log in?
items.answer: Click "Login" in the top-right corner and authorize with your Google account. Your learning record is created automatically on first login.

items.question: Will my learning progress be saved?
items.answer: Yes. All Tutorial views and Practice results sync to the cloud in real time. You can continue where you left off on any device as long as you're signed in.

items.question: What's the difference between Tutorial and Practice?
items.answer: Tutorial covers concepts with animated visualizations. Practice tests your understanding through problems with instant feedback and explanations.

items.question: What's the difference between Algorithm Lab and Visualization Playground?
items.answer: Algorithm Lab lets you run algorithms and compare performance (time complexity, steps). Playground is a free-form explorer: paste your code to see its graph structure and AI analysis.

items.question: What are the limitations of AI analysis?
items.answer: AI recognition supports common sorting, searching, and graph algorithms. Deeply nested recursion or non-standard APIs lower accuracy. Results are for reference only.

items.question: What should I do if execution times out or results look wrong?
items.answer: The sandbox has a 5-second limit. Try optimizing complexity or reducing input size. If the issue persists, please report it via the feedback form below.

items.question: How do I use the map on the Learning Dashboard?
items.answer: The map shows all algorithm topics as a skill tree. Node color indicates progress (not started / in progress / completed). Click a node to jump to its Tutorial or Practice.

items.question: How do I report a bug or give feedback?
items.answer: Fill in the Google Form via the "Feedback" link in the footer, or email codepulse112@gmail.com. Include your browser version and steps to reproduce for faster resolution.
